dissection.title: The theory and practice of the proportionality test in legislative decision-making

theory.evidence: The doctrine of proportionality is central to human rights adjudication
it is most compelling under a deliberative model of democracy
As an expression of the ‘culture of justification’

constructs.evidence: The doctrine of proportionality is central to human rights adjudication
legitimacy depends on transparency, deliberation, and accountability

design.evidence: combining theoretical analysis with case studies

sample.evidence: case studies from the United Kingdom’s weak-form model of rights protection

data.evidence: Empirically, the thesis analyses the Nationality and Borders Bill and the Police, Crime, Sentencing and Courts Bill

findings.text: proportionality’s value depends on structured application
findings.evidence: proportionality’s value depends on structured application
in practice it was found often to operate rhetorically, at times undermining rights protection

future.text: reforms to the House of Commons
future.evidence: reforms to the House of Commons through electoral adjustments, pre-legislative scrutiny, and committee reform offer the most promising path

notes: The text does not provide information on the research methodology used.
The text does not provide information on the software used for analysis.
